title: Cursed Art Effects
entries: Whoever looks upon it becomes convinced they must own it, and will scheme to do so.
The viewer cannot turn right until the curse is lifted.
The viewer's reflection begins to lag, then to act on its own.
Each dawn, the viewer ages a year — until the art is destroyed or hidden from the sun.
The viewer dreams of a burning lake, and each night instinctively knows the way to it.
The viewer can no longer perceive one specific person, as if they were never there.
The viewer must close every door they pass through, even in a hurry.
The viewer's shadow slowly detaches over a tenday and begins to wander off.
The viewer speaks only in the last word of each sentence, echoing softer each time.
Cats hiss and flee from the viewer; any scratch they leave festers and will not heal.
The viewer weeps a single tear of paint whenever they lie.
The viewer's own portrait appears, unbidden, in every mirror they pass.
The viewer hears polite applause whenever they fail at something.
The viewer can no longer bear to be seen — every gaze feels like a brushstroke.
Anything the viewer draws or writes comes true, badly, once.
The viewer's belongings slowly recolour to match the palette of the artwork.
The viewer is compelled to narrate the scene aloud, as a docent would, to anyone nearby.
The viewer ceases to appear in any new artwork, portrait, or reflection.
The viewer must add one small detail to the piece each day, or take 1d4 psychic damage.
Every room the viewer sleeps in is found, by morning, rearranged to match the painting.
